ORDER RE: (DEFENDANTS DAVID CHOO AND CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC.) MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF JOSEPH ZADIK'S MARCH 12, 2009, COMPLAINT (OFF CALENDAR, RE HEARING ON 07/08/09)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DAVID CHOO, CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC., CMR MORTGAGE FUND II, LLC HEARING ON AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION. HEARING HELD AND ARGUED. THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: MATTER SUBMITTED AS TO ARGUMENT. PLAINTIFF'S ORAL REQUEST FOR A STATEMENT OF DECISION PURSUANT TO CCP 1291 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN ISSUES TO BE ADDRESSED IN STATEMENT OF DECISION; AND WRITTEN OBJECTIONS TO EVID. ARE DUE 3/15/2010; DEFENDANT TO PREPARE PROPOSED STATEMENT OF DECISION WHICH IS DUE 4/1/2010; MR. BORNSTEIN COMMENTS AND OTHER PROPOSALS REGARDING PROPOSED STATEMENT OF DECISION DUE 4/16/2010. THE COURT WILL ISSUE A TENTATIVE STATEMENT OF DECISION, THEN COUNSEL WILL HAVE TIME TO REPLY AND THEREAFTER THE COURT WILL ISSUE ITS FINAL STATEMENT OF DECISION. ALL BRIEFINGS TO BE SENT TO THE COURT ON A DISK, IN WORD FORMAT. ANY REQUEST FOR SEALING CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION NEEDS TO BE ACCOMPLISHED THROUGH AN EX PARTE APPLICATION AT THE EX PARTE HEARING, OR THROUGH NOTICED MOTION. Off Calendar Mar-11-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jeffery D. Trowbridge, Esq., for defendant and moving party: David Choo
Jonathan H. Borstein, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Joseph Zadik

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANTS DAVID CHOO, CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC. and CMR MORTGAGE FUND II, LLC's Motion To Quash Or Modify Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum Served On Craig Raymond is granted as the Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum is quashed. Late opposition was not considered. Order signed in open court. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Mar-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DAVID CHOO, CALIFORNIA MORTGAGE AND REALTY, INC., CMR MORTGAGE FUND II, LLC Hearing On Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration and to Stay Action. Appearances: Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein (415) 409-7611, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jeffrey D. Trowbridge, Esq. Attorney at Law, (510) 893-5300 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Hearing held and argued. The Court rules as follows: Matter submitted as to argument. Plaintiff's oral request for a Statement of Decision pursuant to CCP 1291 is granted. Plaintiff's written issues to be addressed in statement of decision; and written objections to Evid. are due 3/15/2010; Defendant to Prepare Proposed Statement of Decision which is due 4/1/2010; Mr. Bornstein comments and other proposals regarding proposed statement of decision due 4/16/2010. The Court will issue a tentative Statement of Decision, then counsel will have time to reply and thereafter the Court will issue its Final Statement of Decision. All briefings to be sent to the Court on a disk, in word format. Any request for sealing confidential information needs to be accomplished through an ex parte application at the ex parte hearing, or through noticed motion. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
